description: Conservative generic emitter family starter entries for Extension 2 exploratory mode. All entries are generic. Values are conservative estimates for trade space exploration only. Do not treat as measured performance or flight qualification.

entries.label: Broadband Radiator (Generic)
notes: Broadband high-emissivity radiator surface representative of well-established spacecraft thermal control coatings (e.g. Z-93, Aeroglaze Z306 class). Nominal emissivity 0.85 is a conservative in-family estimate.
Selectivity fraction = 0 by definition: this surface emits broadly across the IR spectrum. Appropriate as the non-selective baseline comparator for Extension 2 trade studies.

entries.label: Selective Emitter (Generic)
notes: Represents the concept of a selective thermal emitter concentrating emission in a near-IR or short-wave IR band relevant to TPV cell bandgap. Rare-earth oxides (Er2O3, Yb2O3) and photonic crystal emitters have demonstrated selective emission in bench experiments.

Selectivity fraction = 0.55 is a conservative generic estimate. Actual performance is highly material, temperature, and fabrication dependent. Do not use for design commitment without spectral measurement of the specific material at operating temperature.

entries.label: Variable Emissivity Surface (Generic)
notes: Represents electrochromic or mechanically variable emissivity surfaces used for spacecraft thermal control. Nominal emissivity 0.60 is a mid-range value for typical variable-emissivity device operating range (0.1–0.85 is common span).
Selectivity fraction = 0 indicates broadband behavior. Variable emissivity is used for thermal regulation, not spectral selectivity. Include when modeling modulated reject surfaces.
